://>://>://>://>Hi, I'm not sure if I understand your question correctly, but I'll try to answer it as best I can. The planning phase in Kanban is done in a different way than in Scrum. In Kanban, the planning is done in a continuous manner, rather than in a timeboxed sprint. This means that work is pulled from the backlog as capacity allows, rather than being committed to a specific sprint. The team is responsible for deciding what work to pull from the backlog and when to pull it, based on their capacity and the priority of the work. This allows for more flexibility and adaptability, as the team can respond to changing priorities and new requirements as they arise. In terms of retrospectives and refinement meetings, they are still important in Kanban, but they are not as formalized as in Scrum. In Kanban, retrospectives are typically done on a regular basis, such as every two weeks or every month, to review the team's performance and identify areas for improvement. Refinement meetings are also done regularly to review the backlog and prioritize work based on the team's capacity and the business value of the work. However, the focus in Kanban is on continuous improvement and continuous flow, rather than on timeboxed sprints. So, while retrospectives and refinement meetings are still important in Kanban, they are not as formalized as in Scrum, and the focus is on continuous improvement and adaptability rather than on timeboxed sprints. I hope this helps! you want to filter out only those that match a specific domain, you can use a regular expression to do so. A regular expression is a pattern that describes a set of strings, and it can be used to match and extract specific parts of a string.

In this case, we want to match email addresses that end with @johnpaulprep.org. We can do this by using the following regular expression:

/^.*@johnpaulprep\.org$/

This regular expression matches any string that starts with any number of characters, followed by an @ symbol, followed by the domain name johnpaulprep.org, followed by any number of characters. The $ symbol at the end of the expression indicates that the match must end at the end of the string.

To use this regular expression in Python, you can use the re module. Here's an example of how you can use it to filter email addresses:

import re

email_addresses = ['john@example.com', 'jane@johnpaulprep.org', 'jim@johndoe.com']

filtered_email_addresses = []

for email_address in email_addresses:
if re.match(r'^.*@johnpaulprep\.org$', email_address):
filtered_email_addresses.append(email_address)

print(filtered_email_addresses)

In this example, we first import the re module. Then, we create a list of email addresses. We then use a for loop to iterate over each email address in the list, and use the re.match() method to check if the email address matches the regular expression. If it does, we add it to a new list called filtered_email_addresses. Finally, we print the filtered_email_addresses list to see the result.

In this case, the output will be:

['jane@johnpaulprep.org']

This means that the email address jane@johnpaulprep.org is the only one that matches the regular expression, and it is the only one that is returned in the filtered_email_addresses list.

When capturing video from a VHS tape using OBS, the frame rate you select will depend on the specific requirements of your project and the type of video you are working with.

The frame rate is the number of frames per second that the video is captured at. The higher the frame rate, the smoother the video will appear, but it will also require more storage space and may result in a larger file size.

If you are working with a VHS tape that has a lower resolution, such as 720p, then it's recommended to capture the video at a lower frame rate, such as 30fps. This will help to reduce the file size and make it easier to work with.

If you are working with a VHS tape that has a higher resolution, such as 1080p, then it's recommended to capture the video at a higher frame rate, such as 60fps. This will help to capture the video at a higher quality and make it easier to work with.

In summary, when capturing video from a VHS tape using OBS, the frame rate you select will depend on the specific requirements of your project and the type of video you are working with. If you are working with a lower resolution VHS tape, then it's recommended to capture the video at a lower frame rate, such as 30fps. If you are working with a higher resolution VHS tape, then it's recommended to capture the video at a higher frame rate, such as 60fps.

Opening shot of a happy child eating a spoonful of Buckwheat Cereal.

Voiceover: "Looking for a breakfast cereal that's delicious and healthy? Buckwheat Cereal is now even better, it's tobacco-free and infused with melatonin to help your little ones get the restful sleep they need."

Shot of the box of Buckwheat Cereal with a "Tobacco-Free and Now with Melatonin" label prominently displayed.

Voiceover: "You can trust that Buckwheat Cereal is safe and healthy for your family. And with the added bonus of melatonin, your kids will be out like a light, leaving you with plenty of time to enjoy yourself."

Closing shot of a group of happy parents partying while their kids nap peacefully in the background.

Voiceover: "Buckwheat Cereal, with melatonin so sweet, it'll help your little ones sleep for hours on end - so you can party all night without having to bend!"

Jingle: "Buckwheat Cereal, delicious and new - with melatonin so sweet, your kids will snooze. Restful sleep for hours on end, so you can enjoy your free time with your friends!"

Use a responsive design approach: A responsive design approach involves designing the UI to adapt to different screen sizes and orientations. This can be achieved by using a combination of flexible layouts, media queries, and responsive widgets.2. Use a responsive layout: A responsive layout is a layout that adjusts its size and position based on the available screen space. This can be achieved by using a combination of flexible widgets, such as `Flexible` and `Expanded`, and `MediaQuery` to retrieve the current screen size.3. Use a responsive bottom sheet: A responsive bottom sheet is a bottom sheet that adjusts its size and position based on the available screen space. Use a responsive snackbar: A responsive snackbar is a snackbar that adjusts its size and position based on the available screen space. This can be achieved by using a combination of `SnackBar` and `MediaQuery` to retrieve the current screen size.13. Use a responsive dialog: A responsive dialog is a dialog that adjusts its size and position based on the available screen space. This can be achieved by using a combination of `Dialog` and `MediaQuery` to retrieve the current screen size.14. ://> Yes, that's correct. The terms "stash" and "repository" are often used in the context of software development, but they have different meanings and connotations.

A "stash" is typically used to refer to a temporary storage area for changes to a project that have not yet been committed to the main codebase. A stash is usually local to the developer's machine, and is not shared with other members of the development team. Stashes are often used when a developer needs to switch to working on a different branch or task, but doesn't want to commit their changes yet.

On the other hand, a "repository" is a central location where the complete history of a project is stored, along with all the files, code, and other assets that make up the project. A repository is usually shared among all members of a development team, who can access, modify, and collaborate on the project files. A repository typically includes version control tools that allow developers to track changes to the project over time, and to collaborate on the same codebase.

In summary, a stash is a temporary storage area for changes that haven't been committed to the main codebase, while a repository is a central location for the entire project, including all its history

Mediawiki uses a combination of the `User` table and the `User_groups` table to store user information, including passwords. The `User` table stores basic user information such as the username, real name, email address, and password hash. The `User_groups` table stores the user's group memberships, which can be used to control access to different parts of the wiki.

The password hash is stored in the `User` table using the `user_password` field. The password hash is generated using the `password_hash()` function in PHP, which uses the Bcrypt algorithm to generate a secure hash. The hash is then stored in the database using the `password_hash()` function.

To set up a new Mediawiki installation, you will need to create a new database and import the Mediawiki database schema. You can then use the `Special:UserLogin` page to create new users and set their passwords. Mediawiki will automatically generate a password hash for each new user and store it in the database.

If you want to use a different password hashing algorithm, you can modify the `LocalSettings.php` file to specify the algorithm to use. However, it's generally recommended to use the default Bcrypt algorithm, as it is a secure and widely used algorithm.

so that the output is a new excel file with a tab for each unique combination of the "CPT" and "BlueHost" columns?
Yes, you can do that by using the `groupby()` function and the `size()` function of the dataframe. Here's an example:
```
import pandas as pd

#Read in the csv file
df = pd.read_csv('your_file.csv')

#Create a new dataframe containing each unique combination of the "CPT" column and the "BlueHost" column
df_grouped = df.groupby(['CPT','BlueHost']).size().reset_index(name='counts')

#This new dataframe will have three columns, "CPT","BlueHost" and "counts"

#Output the filtered dataframe to a new excel file with a specified tab name
df_grouped.to_excel('filtered_data.xlsx', sheet_name='your_tab_name', index=False)
```
You will have to replace 'your\_file.csv' with the name of your csv file and 'your\_tab\_name' with the tab name you want the sheet to have in the excel file. This new dataframe will have three columns, "CPT","BlueHost" and "counts" where "counts" column tells you the number of rows for each unique combination of the "CPT" and "BlueHost" columns.

The Digispark board uses the V-USB software USB stack to emulate a USB keyboard, which means that it does not have a bootloader. Instead, it uses the V-USB library to communicate with the computer as if it were a USB keyboard.

When the board is powered on, it enters the V-USB library's initialization routine, which sets up the USB interface and configures the board to act as a USB keyboard. The V-USB library then handles all communication with the computer, including sending keystrokes and receiving data from the computer.

Because the Digispark board does not have a bootloader, it cannot be detected as an Arduino device during the bootloader stage. Instead, it is detected as a USB keyboard, and the V-USB library handles all communication with the computer.
What is the difference between DigiKeyboard.h and Keyboard.h?

Sure thing! Here's a shopping list for the ingredients needed to make two cakes:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1 cup granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 2 large eggs
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1 cup milk
* 1/2 cup vegetable oil
* 1/2 cup cocoa powder
* 1/2 cup hot water
* 1/2 cup chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup chopped nuts (optional)
* 1/2 cup powdered sugar
* 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/4 cup heavy cream

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
4. In a separate bowl, cream together the sugar and butter until light and fluffy.
5. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Stir in the vanilla extract.
7. Alternately add the dry ingredients and milk to the creamed mixture, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients.
8. Pour half of the batter into each prepared pan.
9. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Let cool in pans for 10 minutes before removing to

and case studies. Explain how to create a UI design system and how to use it in the design process. Explain how to create a UI design system and how to use it in the design process. A UI design system is a set of design guidelines, components, and patterns that are used to create a consistent and cohesive user interface across different digital products. It helps to ensure that the design is consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain.

To create a UI design system, you need to start by defining the design principles and guidelines that will be used to create the system. This includes things like color palette, typography, spacing, and other visual elements. You also need to create a library of design components that can be used to build the user interface. These components can include things like buttons, forms, icons, and other UI elements.

Once you have created the design system, you need to use it in the design process. This means using the design components and guidelines to create the user interface for your digital product. You should also ensure that the design system is scalable and can be used across different devices and platforms.

To use the UI design system effectively, you need to follow a few best practices. These include:

* Using the design system consistently across all digital products
* Keeping the design system up-to-date and evolving with the latest design trends and technologies
* Testing the design system to ensure that it is user-friendly and accessible
* Collaborating with other designers and stakeholders to ensure that the design system meets the business goals and objectives.

By following these best practices, you can create a UI design system that is effective and helps to create a consistent and cohesive user interface across different digital products.

## Answer

You can use a generic function:
```
func sql<T>(query: String, transformation: @escaping ([PostgresqlRow]) -> [T]) -> [T] {
    // ...
}
```
Then you can call it like this:
```
sql("select * from items") { $0.map { ItemTable.from(row: $0) } }
```
